name: CO2 radiative forcing — logarithmic concentration dependence
principles: infrared band saturation of CO2 absorption lines
logarithmic forcing as a consequence of band-wing scaling
conventions: Delta F is the instantaneous top-of-atmosphere radiative forcing (W/m^2) due to changing CO2 concentration
C is the current atmospheric CO2 mixing ratio (ppm); C_0 is a reference, typically pre-industrial 280 ppm
alpha ~ 5.35 W/m^2 is the empirical coefficient (Myhre et al. 1998); IPCC AR6 uses a slightly refined form including N2O and CH4 cross-terms
doubling CO2 (C / C_0 = 2): Delta F ~ 5.35 * ln 2 ~ 3.71 W/m^2 (the standard '2xCO2' radiative forcing)
valid for clear-sky, all-sky averages; cloud feedbacks and ice-albedo feedbacks are SEPARATE corrections, not in this expression
logarithmic form holds in the moderate-concentration regime (~50-1000 ppm); breaks down at very low or very high CO2
expectedLimits: C / C_0 = 2 (doubling):  Delta F ~ 3.71 W/m^2 (canonical climate-sensitivity benchmark)
C / C_0 = 4 (quadrupling):  Delta F ~ 7.42 W/m^2 (logarithmic, so each doubling adds the same amount)
C -> 0:  Delta F -> -infinity formally; physically, the absorption lines unsaturate and the linear-in-concentration regime kicks in
C very large (>> 1000 ppm):  band-wing scaling breaks down; deviation from log law; full line-by-line radiative transfer required
equilibrium climate sensitivity (ECS) = (Delta F) / lambda where lambda is the climate-feedback parameter; ECS for 2xCO2 forcing is the canonical 1.5-4.5 K range (IPCC AR6)
references: Myhre, Highwood, Shine & Stordal (1998) — New estimates of radiative forcing due to well mixed greenhouse gases, Geophys. Res. Lett.
IPCC AR6 WG1 — Chapter 7, Table 7.SM.1 (radiative forcing parameterisations)
Pierrehumbert — Principles of Planetary Climate, ch.4
